summary: The test suite comprehensively covers all four public functions (Get-FileHashMap, Compare-DirectoryTrees, New-SyncPlan, Invoke-SyncPlan) plus error handling and edge cases. All task requirements — hash-based comparison, source-only/target-only/modified/identical detection, dry-run vs execute mode, nested directories, and error propagation — have corresponding tests. Rigor is strong: empty files, binary content, spaces in filenames, empty-tree scenarios, and a post-sync re-comparison to verify idempotent correctness are all covered. However, the failure path (the 'Failed' status branch in Invoke-SyncPlan when a file operation throws) is never exercised, and there are no tests for edge cases like read-only target files or source disappearing between plan creation and execution. Design is clean: GUID-based temp dirs prevent inter-test collision, BeforeEach/AfterEach reliably manage fixtures, $script:-scoped variables are used correctly, and error-handling tests use try/finally for cleanup. Minor brittleness: one test hard-codes a SHA-256 value that is encoding-sensitive (Set-Content -NoNewline encoding may vary by platform), and the mixed-scenario test uses Should -Contain without asserting total counts for each category, leaving room for silent extra entries. Overall a high-quality suite that would reliably catch regressions across the core sync logic.
